title: Figure 1 from: Laksmiani NPL, Widiantara IWA, Pawarrangan ABS (2022) Potency of moringa (Moringa oleifera L.) leaves extract containing quercetin as a depigmentation agent inhibiting the tyrosinase enzyme using in-silico and in-vitro assay. Pharmacia 69(1): 85-92. https://doi.org/10.3897/pharmacia.69.e73132
author: Laksmiani, Ni Putu Linda
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Figure 1 Redocking visualization between tyrosinase enzyme and native ligand. 1: The structure of the tyrosinase enzyme (PDB ID: 2Y9X), 2a: tyrosinase enzyme structure without native ligand, 2b: native ligand (tropolone) structure, 3a: redocking conformation, 3b: 3D interaction of amino acid residues from tyrosinase enzyme with native ligand, 3c: 2D interaction of amino acid residues from tyrosinase enzyme with native ligand.
steps_raw: Figure 1 Redocking visualization between tyrosinase enzyme and native ligand. 1: The structure of the tyrosinase enzyme (PDB ID: 2Y9X), 2a: tyrosinase enzyme structure without native ligand, 2b: native ligand (tropolone) structure, 3a: redocking conformation, 3b: 3D interaction of amino acid residues from tyrosinase enzyme with native ligand, 3c: 2D interaction of amino acid residues from tyrosinase enzyme with native ligand.
